pi_items.metadata_value: Instructional Coordinator - preparing offline training materials
pi_items.verified_value: Instructional Coordinator - preparing offline training materials
pi_items.source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics defines instructional coordinators as professionals who develop and implement instructional materials and training. Task context of offline training for rural school aligns with typical coordinator responsibilities.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real, publicly accessible Wikipedia articles: Bloom's taxonomy (217.6 KB HTML), Constructivism in philosophy of education (290.1 KB HTML), and Differentiated instruction (208.4 KB HTML). All three articles are substantial and when converted to PDF format will exceed the 20 KB minimum size requirement (estimated 62-145 KB each). The target directory is /home/ga/Documents/Workshop_Materials with required files blooms_taxonomy.pdf, constructivism.pdf, differentiated_instruction.pdf, and a README.txt index file. The occupation context of instructional coordinator preparing offline training materials for a rural school is authentic and consistent with occupational definitions.
